_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**1. SINIR MTR integration in transport subcontracts (in force; gap likely exists)**
All Brazilian road transport subcontractors used by the workspace to move goods that include or become solid waste (packaging materials, spent crating, event consumables, automotive transport packaging) must be verified as SINIR MTR-registered. The workspace must insert a contractual clause in all Brazilian haulier agreements requiring the counterparty to (a) hold active SINIR MTR registration or applicable state-system registration; (b) issue a valid MTR for every waste movement; and (c) provide CDFs upon request. This is a contract-amendment task with a 30-day target. The workspace must also build a document-retention system for MTRs and CDFs linked to each Brazilian shipment file — the MTR must accompany the load throughout transport and the CDF is issued post-receipt by the treating/recycling facility. Baseline gap: standard freight forwarding TMS systems do not natively capture MTR/CDF data fields; a workflow addition or document-upload module is required.

**2. PGRS determination and annual SINIR inventory reporting (annual cycle; assessment needed now)**
If the workspace operates or manages any Brazilian warehouse, bonded facility, or consolidation point that generates industrial solid waste above statutory thresholds, it must prepare and maintain a PGRS and submit an annual national solid waste inventory to SINIR by 31 March each year. The inventory covers waste type, volume, classification under ABNT NBR 10.004, storage method, and final destination. The workspace should obtain an environmental law opinion on whether its Brazilian operational footprint (if any) triggers this obligation. If triggered, an operational data-collection process must be in place throughout the calendar year to enable 31 March filing; ad hoc compilation after year-end is insufficient.

**3. Supplier/partner onboarding — reverse logistics compliance verification**
For each regulated product category handled by the workspace on behalf of importers (batteries in live-event and film/TV equipment, electronic components, fluorescent lighting, lubricants for automotive shipments), the workspace must add a reverse logistics compliance verification step to supplier/partner onboarding. This means requiring evidence that the importer of record has an active SINIR-registered reverse logistics system (individual or through a collective sectoral agreement) before accepting the logistics mandate. This is a due-diligence and intake-workflow change, not a capital expenditure, but requires staff training on the six statutory product categories and on how to verify SINIR registration status.

**4. Plastic packaging compliance system (Decree 12,688/2025 — operative from Jan 2026)**
The workspace must assess whether the secondary and tertiary plastic packaging it uses in Brazilian operations (crating wraps, pallet shrink film, polystyrene protectors used for fine art and luxury goods) constitutes packaging 'placed on the market' in Brazil by an entity for which it is the importer of record. If yes, the workspace must: (a) determine whether to join a collective reverse logistics system or establish an individual one; (b) register the chosen model and system participants; (c) install measurement capability for mass of plastic packaging placed on market by region; and (d) build annual reporting workflows to SINIR. The recycled content obligation (22% from Jan 2026 for large companies) additionally requires documentation and traceability of recycled material content — a procurement and specification change that touches packaging procurement and supplier contracts.

**5. Staff training**
Operational and compliance staff handling Brazilian trade lanes must be trained on: (a) the six statutory reverse logistics product categories and what triggers importer obligation; (b) MTR and CDF document types, issuance workflow, and retention requirements; (c) SINIR registration verification for transport partners; and (d) the plastic packaging decree obligations. Training should be completed within 60 days and documented.

**6. Reference document update**
All internal PGRS templates, transport contracts, waste-disposal agreements, and operational manuals referencing Decree 7,404/2010 must be updated to reference Decree 10,936/2022 as the operative instrument. Failure to update creates audit liability.

*Source: Decreto Federal n.º 10,936/2022, Presidência da República, 12 January 2022. https://www.planalto.gov.br/ccivil_03/_ato2019-2022/2022/decreto/d10936.htm; SINIR MTR platform: https://sinir.gov.br/sistemas/mtr/; Decreto n.º 12,688/2025; Baker McKenzie analysis, January 2026: https://www.bakermckenzie.com/en/insight/publications/2026/01/brazil-implications-of-the-new-plastics-decree*
